metadata.summary: Apache Chemistry CMIS client library for Python

documentation:
ABOUT

Thanks for using cmislib, the CMIS client library for Python.

The goal of this library is to provide an interoperable API to CMIS
repositories such as Alfresco, Nuxeo, KnowledgeTree, MS SharePoint,
EMC Documentum, and any other content repository that is CMIS-compliant.

More info on CMIS can be found at:
http://www.oasis-open.org/committees/cmis

SOURCE

The source code for this project lives at http://chemistry.apache.org/

TESTS

There are unit tests available in the tests directory. They require access
to a CMIS provider. There are many freely-available CMIS repositories
available to run locally or that are hosted.

DOC

Documentation that tells you what this is all about can be found in the doc
directory. Please see the doc for dependencies, required CMIS version level,
required Python version, etc.

classes.name: cmislib.model.CmisClient

methods.name: cmislib.model.CmisClient.__init__
documentation:
This is the entry point to the API. You need to know the
\:param repositoryUrl: The service URL of the CMIS provider
\:param username: Username
\:param password: Password

```pycon
>>> client = CmisClient(
...    'http://localhost:8080/alfresco/s/cmis', 'admin', 'admin')
```

documentation: To string

methods.name: cmislib.model.CmisClient._check_response_status
documentation:
A callback function called by 'requests' after a call to the cmis
container
\:param response: the response object
\:param ags:
\:param kwargs:
\:return:

methods.name: cmislib.model.CmisClient.getRepositories
documentation:
Returns a dict of high-level info about the repositories available at
this service. The dict contains entries for 'repositoryId' and
'repositoryName'.

```pycon
>>> client.getRepositories()
[{'repositoryName': u'Main Repository',
  'repositoryId': u'83beb297-a6fa-4ac5-844b-98c871c0eea9'}]
```

methods.name: cmislib.model.CmisClient.getRepository
documentation:
Returns the repository identified by the specified repositoryId.

```pycon
>>> repo = client.getRepository('83beb297-a6fa-4ac5-844b-98c871c0eea9')
>>> repo.getRepositoryName()
u'Main Repository'
```

methods.name: cmislib.model.CmisClient.getDefaultRepository
documentation:
There does not appear to be anything in the spec that identifies
a repository as being the default, so we'll define it to be the
first one in the list.

```pycon
>>> repo = client.getDefaultRepository()
>>> repo.getRepositoryId()
u'83beb297-a6fa-4ac5-844b-98c871c0eea9'
```
Handles all communication with the CMIS provider.

methods.name: cmislib.cmis_services.Binding.getRepositoryService
documentation: Returns the repository service specific to this binding.

methods.name: cmislib.cmis_services.Binding.get
documentation:
Does a get against the CMIS service. More than likely, you will not
need to call this method. Instead, let the other objects do it for you.

For example, if you need to get a specific object by object id, try
{class}`Repository.getObject`. If you have a path instead of an object
id, use {class}`Repository.getObjectByPath`. Or, you could start with
the root folder ({class}`Repository.getRootFolder`) and drill down from
there.

methods.name: cmislib.cmis_services.Binding.post
documentation:
Does a post against the CMIS service. More than likely, you will not
need to call this method. Instead, let the other objects do it for you.

For example, to update the properties on an object, you'd call
{class}`CmisObject.updateProperties`. Or, to check in a document that's
been checked out, you'd call {class}`Document.checkin` on the PWC.

methods.name: cmislib.cmis_services.Binding.delete
documentation:
Does a delete against the CMIS service. More than likely, you will not
need to call this method. Instead, let the other objects do it for you.

For example, to delete a folder you'd call {class}`Folder.delete` and
to delete a document you'd call {class}`Document.delete`.

methods.name: cmislib.cmis_services.Binding.put
documentation:
Does a put against the CMIS service. More than likely, you will not
need to call this method. Instead, let the other objects do it for you.

For example, to update the properties on an object, you'd call
{class}`CmisObject.updateProperties`. Or, to check in a document that's
been checked out, you'd call {class}`Document.checkin` on the PWC.

methods.name: cmislib.cmis_services.Binding._processCommonErrors
documentation:
Maps HTTPErrors that are common to all to exceptions. Only errors
that are truly global, like 401 not authorized, should be handled
here. Callers should handle the rest.
Represents the binding used to communicate with the CMIS server.

classes.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.__str__
documentation: To string

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.reload
documentation:
Fetches the latest representation of this object from the CMIS service.
Some methods, like {class}`^Document.checkout` do this for you.

If you call reload with a properties filter, the filter will be in
effect on subsequent calls until the filter argument is changed. To
reset to the full list of properties, call reload with filter set to
'\*'.

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getObjectId
documentation:
Returns the object ID for this object.

```pycon
>>> doc = resultSet.getResults()[0]
>>> doc.getObjectId()
u'workspace://SpacesStore/dc26102b-e312-471b-b2af-91bfb0225339'
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getObjectParents
documentation:
Gets the parents of this object as a {class}`ResultSet`.

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - filter
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - includeRelativePathSegment

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getPaths
documentation: Returns the object's paths as a list of strings.

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getAllowableActions
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of allowable actions, keyed off of the action
name.

```pycon
>>> actions = doc.getAllowableActions()
>>> for a in actions:
...     print "%s:%s" % (a,actions[a])
...
canDeleteContentStream:True
canSetContentStream:True
canCreateRelationship:True
canCheckIn:False
canApplyACL:False
canDeleteObject:True
canGetAllVersions:True
canGetObjectParents:True
canGetProperties:True
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getTitle
documentation: Returns the value of the object's cmis:title property.

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getProperties
documentation:
Returns a dict of the object's properties. If CMIS returns an
empty element for a property, the property will be in the
dict with a value of None.

```pycon
>>> props = doc.getProperties()
>>> for p in props:
...     print "%s: %s" % (p, props[p])
...
cmis:contentStreamMimeType: text/html
cmis:creationDate: 2009-12-15T09:45:35.369-06:00
cmis:baseTypeId: cmis:document
cmis:isLatestMajorVersion: false
cmis:isImmutable: false
cmis:isMajorVersion: false
cmis:objectId:
    workspace://SpacesStore/dc26102b-e312-471b-b2af-91bfb0225339
```

The optional filter argument is not yet implemented.

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getName
documentation:
Returns the value of cmis:name from the getProperties() dictionary.
We don't need a getter for every standard CMIS property, but name
is a pretty common one so it seems to make sense.

```pycon
>>> doc.getName()
u'system-overview.html'
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.updateProperties
documentation:
Updates the properties of an object with the properties provided.
Only provide the set of properties that need to be updated.

```pycon
>>> folder = repo.getObjectByPath('/someFolder2')
>>> folder.getName()
u'someFolder2'
>>> props = {'cmis:name': 'someFolderFoo'}
>>> folder.updateProperties(props)
<cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder object at 0x103ab1210>
>>> folder.getName()
u'someFolderFoo'
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.move
documentation:
Moves an object from the source folder to the target folder.

```pycon
>>> sub1 = repo.getObjectByPath('/cmislib/sub1')
>>> sub2 = repo.getObjectByPath('/cmislib/sub2')
>>> doc = repo.getObjectByPath('/cmislib/sub1/testdoc1')
>>> doc.move(sub1, sub2)
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.delete
documentation:
Deletes this {class}`CmisObject` from the repository. Note that in the
case of a {class}`Folder` object, some repositories will refuse to
delete it if it contains children and some will delete it without
complaint. If what you really want to do is delete the folder and all
of its descendants, use {meth}`~Folder.deleteTree` instead.

```pycon
>>> folder.delete()
```

The optional allVersions argument is supported.

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.applyPolicy
documentation: This is not yet implemented.

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.createRelationship
documentation:
Creates a relationship between this object and a specified target
object using the relationship type specified. Returns the new
{class}`Relationship` object.

```pycon
>>> rel = tstDoc1.createRelationship(tstDoc2, 'R:cmiscustom:assoc')
>>> rel.getProperties()
{u'cmis:objectId':
     u'workspace://SpacesStore/271c48dd-6548-4771-a8f5-0de69b7cdc25',
 u'cmis:creationDate': None,
 u'cmis:objectTypeId': u'R:cmiscustom:assoc',
 u'cmis:lastModificationDate': None,
 u'cmis:targetId':
     u'workspace://SpacesStore/0ca1aa08-cb49-42e2-8881-53aa8496a1c1',
 u'cmis:lastModifiedBy': None,
 u'cmis:baseTypeId': u'cmis:relationship',
 u'cmis:sourceId':
     u'workspace://SpacesStore/271c48dd-6548-4771-a8f5-0de69b7cdc25',
 u'cmis:changeToken': None,
 u'cmis:createdBy': None}
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getRelationships
documentation:
Returns a {class}`ResultSet` of {class}`Relationship` objects for each
relationship where the source is this object.

```pycon
>>> rels = tstDoc1.getRelationships()
>>> len(rels.getResults())
1
>>> rel = rels.getResults().values()[0]
>>> rel.getProperties()
{u'cmis:objectId':
     u'workspace://SpacesStore/271c48dd-6548-4771-a8f5-0de69b7cdc25',
 u'cmis:creationDate': None,
 u'cmis:objectTypeId': u'R:cmiscustom:assoc',
 u'cmis:lastModificationDate': None,
 u'cmis:targetId':
     u'workspace://SpacesStore/0ca1aa08-cb49-42e2-8881-53aa8496a1c1',
 u'cmis:lastModifiedBy': None,
 u'cmis:baseTypeId': u'cmis:relationship',
 u'cmis:sourceId':
     u'workspace://SpacesStore/271c48dd-6548-4771-a8f5-0de69b7cdc25',
 u'cmis:changeToken': None,
 u'cmis:createdBy': None}
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - includeSubRelationshipTypes
  - relationshipDirection
  - typeId
  - maxItems
  - skipCount
  - filter
  - includeAllowableActions

documentation: This is not yet implemented.

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getAppliedPolicies

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getACL
documentation:
Repository.getCapabilities['ACL'] must return manage or discover.

```pycon
>>> acl = folder.getACL()
>>> acl.getEntries()
{u'GROUP_EVERYONE':
     <cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubACE object at 0x10071a8d0>,
 'jdoe': <cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubACE object at 0x10071a590>}
```

The optional onlyBasicPermissions argument is currently not supported.

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.applyACL
documentation:
Updates the object with the provided {class}`ACL`.
Repository.getCapabilities['ACL'] must return manage to invoke this
call.

```pycon
>>> acl = folder.getACL()
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jdoe', 'cmis:write', 'true'))
>>> acl.getEntries()
{u'GROUP_EVERYONE':
     <cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubACE object at 0x10071a8d0>,
 'jdoe': <cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubACE object at 0x10071a590>}
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.CmisObject.getRepository
documentation: Returns the object's repository

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Repository.getRepositoryInfo
documentation:
Returns a dict of repository information.

```pycon
>>> repo = client.getDefaultRepository()>>> repo.getRepositoryName()
u'Main Repository'
>>> info = repo.getRepositoryInfo()
>>> for k,v in info.items():
...     print "%s:%s" % (k,v)
...
cmisSpecificationTitle:Version 1.0 Committee Draft 04
cmisVersionSupported:1.0
repositoryDescription:None
productVersion:3.2.0 (r2 2440)
rootFolderId:
    workspace://SpacesStore/aa1ecedf-9551-49c5-831a-0502bb43f348
repositoryId:83beb297-a6fa-4ac5-844b-98c871c0eea9
repositoryName:Main Repository
vendorName:Alfresco
productName:Alfresco Repository (Community)
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Repository.getObjectByPath
documentation:
Returns an object given the path to the object.

```pycon
>>> doc = repo.getObjectByPath('/jeff test/sample-b.pdf')
>>> doc.getTitle()
u'sample-b.pdf'
```

The following optional arguments are not currently supported:
: - filter
  - includeAllowableActions

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Repository.getSupportedPermissions
documentation:
Returns the value of the cmis:supportedPermissions element. Valid
values are:

> - basic: indicates that the CMIS Basic permissions are supported
> - repository: indicates that repository specific permissions are
>   : supported
> - both: indicates that both CMIS basic permissions and repository
>   : specific permissions are supported

```pycon
>>> repo.supportedPermissions
u'both'
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Repository.getPermissionDefinitions
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of permission definitions for this repository. The
key is the permission string or technical name of the permission
and the value is the permission description.

```pycon
>>> for permDef in repo.permissionDefinitions:
...     print permDef
...
cmis:all
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.LinkChildren
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/content/1.0}folder.Consumer
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/security/1.0}All.All
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.CreateAssociations
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.FullControl
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.AddChildren
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.ReadAssociations
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/content/1.0}folder.Editor
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/content/1.0}cmobject.Editor
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.DeleteAssociations
cmis:read
cmis:write
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Repository.getPermissionMap
documentation:
Returns a dictionary representing the permission mapping table where
each key is a permission key string and each value is a list of one or
more permissions the principal must have to perform the operation.

```pycon
>>> for (k,v) in repo.permissionMap.items():
...     print 'To do this: %s, you must have these perms:' % k
...     for perm in v:
...             print perm
...
To do this: canCreateFolder.Folder, you must have these perms:
cmis:all
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.CreateChildren
To do this: canAddToFolder.Folder, you must have these perms:
cmis:all
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.CreateChildren
To do this: canDelete.Object, you must have these perms:
cmis:all
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.DeleteNode
To do this: canCheckin.Document, you must have these perms:
cmis:all
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/content/1.0}lockable.CheckIn
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Repository.query
documentation:
Returns a list of {class}`CmisObject` objects based on the CMIS
Query Language passed in as the statement. The actual objects
returned will be instances of the appropriate child class based
on the object's base type ID.

In order for the results to be properly instantiated as objects,
make sure you include 'cmis:objectId' as one of the fields in
your select statement, or just use "SELECT ".

If you want the search results to automatically be instantiated with
the appropriate sub-class of {class}`CmisObject` you must either
include cmis:baseTypeId as one of the fields in your select statement
or just use "SELECT ".

```pycon
>>> q = "select * from cmis:document where cmis:name like '%test%'"
>>> resultSet = repo.query(q)
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
1
>>> resultSet.hasNext()
False
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - searchAllVersions
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - maxItems
  - skipCount

```pycon
>>> q = 'select * from cmis:document'
>>> rs = repo.query(q)
>>> len(rs.getResults())
148
>>> rs = repo.query(q, maxItems='5')
>>> len(rs.getResults())
5
>>> rs.hasNext()
True
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Repository.createDocumentFromString
documentation:
Creates a new document setting the content to the string provided. If
the repository supports unfiled objects, you do not have to pass in
a parent {class}`Folder` otherwise it is required.

This method is essentially a convenience method that wraps your string
with a StringIO and then calls createDocument.

```pycon
>>> repo.createDocumentFromString(
...    'testdoc5', parentFolder=testFolder,
...    contentString='Hello, World!', contentType='text/plain')
<cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument object at 0x101352ed0>
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Repository.createDocument
documentation:
Creates a new {class}`Document` object. If the repository
supports unfiled objects, you do not have to pass in
a parent {class}`Folder` otherwise it is required.

To create a document with an associated contentFile, pass in a
File object. The method will attempt to guess the appropriate content
type and encoding based on the file. To specify it yourself, pass them
in via the contentType and contentEncoding arguments.

```pycon
>>> f = open('sample-a.pdf', 'rb')
>>> doc = folder.createDocument('sample-a.pdf', contentFile=f)
<cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument object at 0x105be5e10>
>>> f.close()
>>> doc.getTitle()
u'sample-a.pdf'
```

The following optional arguments are not currently supported:
: - versioningState
  - policies
  - addACEs
  - removeACEs

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Repository.getCollection
documentation:
Returns a list of objects returned for the specified collection.

If the query collection is requested, an exception will be raised.
That collection isn't meant to be retrieved.

If the types collection is specified, the method returns the result of
`getTypeDefinitions` and ignores any optional params passed in.

```pycon
>>> from cmislib.atompub.atompub_binding import TYPES_COLL
>>> types = repo.getCollection(TYPES_COLL)
>>> len(types)
4
>>> types[0].getTypeId()
u'cmis:folder'
```

Otherwise, the collection URL is invoked, and a {class}`ResultSet` is
returned.

```pycon
>>> from cmislib.atompub.atompub_binding import CHECKED_OUT_COLL
>>> resultSet = repo.getCollection(CHECKED_OUT_COLL)
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
1
```

Represents a CMIS repository. Will lazily populate itself by
calling the repository CMIS service URL.

You must pass in an instance of a CmisClient when creating an
instance of this class.

classes.name: cmislib.domain.Document

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Document.checkout
documentation:
Performs a checkout on the {class}`Document` and returns the
Private Working Copy (PWC), which is also an instance of
{class}`Document`

```pycon
>>> doc.getObjectId()
u'workspace://SpacesStore/f0c8b90f-bec0-4405-8b9c-2ab570589808;1.0'
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
>>> pwc = doc.checkout()
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
True
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Document.cancelCheckout
documentation:
Cancels the checkout of this object by retrieving the Private Working
Copy (PWC) and then deleting it. After the PWC is deleted, this object
will be reloaded to update properties related to a checkout.

```pycon
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
True
>>> doc.cancelCheckout()
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Document.getPrivateWorkingCopy
documentation:
Retrieves the object using the object ID in the property:
cmis:versionSeriesCheckedOutId then uses getObject to instantiate
the object.

```pycon
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
>>> doc.checkout()
<cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument object at 0x103a25ad0>
>>> pwc = doc.getPrivateWorkingCopy()
>>> pwc.getTitle()
u'sample-b (Working Copy).pdf'
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Document.isCheckedOut
documentation:
Returns true if the document is checked out.

```pycon
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
True
>>> doc.cancelCheckout()
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Document.getCheckedOutBy
documentation:
Returns the ID who currently has the document checked out.
\>>> pwc = doc.checkout()
\>>> pwc.getCheckedOutBy()
u'admin'

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Document.checkin
documentation:
Checks in this {class}`Document` which must be a private
working copy (PWC).

```pycon
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
>>> pwc = doc.checkout()
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
True
>>> pwc.checkin()
<cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument object at 0x103a8ae90>
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Document.getLatestVersion

```pycon
>>> latestDoc = doc.getLatestVersion()

u'2.1'
>>> latestDoc = doc.getLatestVersion(major='false')
>>> latestDoc.getProperties()['cmis:versionLabel']
u'2.1'
>>> latestDoc = doc.getLatestVersion(major='true')
>>> latestDoc.getProperties()['cmis:versionLabel']
u'2.0'
```

classes.name: cmislib.domain.Folder

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Folder.createFolder
documentation:
Creates a new {class}`Folder` using the properties provided.
Right now I expect a property called 'cmis:name' but I don't
complain if it isn't there (although the CMIS provider will). If a
cmis:name property isn't provided, the value passed in to the name
argument will be used.

To specify a custom folder type, pass in a property called
cmis:objectTypeId set to the {class}`CmisId` representing the type ID
of the instance you want to create. If you do not pass in an object
type ID, an instance of 'cmis:folder' will be created.

```pycon
>>> subFolder = folder.createFolder('someSubfolder')
>>> subFolder.getName()
u'someSubfolder'
```

The following optional arguments are not supported:
: - policies
  - addACEs
  - removeACEs

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Folder.createDocumentFromString
documentation:
Creates a new document setting the content to the string provided. If
the repository supports unfiled objects, you do not have to pass in
a parent {class}`Folder` otherwise it is required.

This method is essentially a convenience method that wraps your string
with a StringIO and then calls createDocument.

```pycon
>>> testFolder.createDocumentFromString(
...    'testdoc3', contentString='hello, world',
...    contentType='text/plain')
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Folder.createDocument
documentation:
Creates a new Document object in the repository using
the properties provided.

Right now this is basically the same as createFolder,
but this deals with contentStreams. The common logic should
probably be moved to CmisObject.createObject.

The method will attempt to guess the appropriate content
type and encoding based on the file. To specify it yourself, pass them
in via the contentType and contentEncoding arguments.

```pycon
>>> f = open('250px-Cmis_logo.png', 'rb')
>>> subFolder.createDocument('logo.png', contentFile=f)
<cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument object at 0x10410fa10>
>>> f.close()
```

If you wanted to set one or more properties when creating the doc, pass
in a dict, like this:

```pycon
>>> props = {'cmis:someProp':'someVal'}
>>> f = open('250px-Cmis_logo.png', 'rb')
>>> subFolder.createDocument('logo.png', props, contentFile=f)
<cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument object at 0x10410fa10>
>>> f.close()
```

To specify a custom object type, pass in a property called
cmis:objectTypeId set to the {class}`CmisId` representing the type ID
of the instance you want to create. If you do not pass in an object
type ID, an instance of 'cmis:document' will be created.

The following optional arguments are not yet supported:
: - versioningState
  - policies
  - addACEs
  - removeACEs

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Folder.getChildren
documentation:
Returns a paged {class}`ResultSet`. The result set contains a list of
{class}`CmisObject` objects for each child of the Folder. The actual
type of the object returned depends on the object's CMIS base type id.
For example, the method might return a list that contains both
{class}`Document` objects and {class}`Folder` objects.

```pycon
>>> childrenRS = subFolder.getChildren()
>>> children = childrenRS.getResults()
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - maxItems
  - skipCount
  - orderBy
  - filter
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - includePathSegment

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Folder.getDescendants
documentation:
Gets the descendants of this folder. The descendants are returned as
a paged {class}`ResultSet` object. The result set contains a list of
{class}`CmisObject` objects where the actual type of each object
returned will vary depending on the object's base type id. For example,
the method might return a list that contains both {class}`Document`
objects and {class}`Folder` objects.

The following optional argument is supported:
: - depth. Use depth=-1 for all descendants, which is the default if no
    depth is specified.

```pycon
>>> resultSet = folder.getDescendants()
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
105
>>> resultSet = folder.getDescendants(depth=1)
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
103
```

The following optional arguments *may* also work but haven't been
tested:

> - filter
> - includeRelationships
> - renditionFilter
> - includeAllowableActions
> - includePathSegment

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Folder.getTree
documentation:
Unlike {class}`Folder.getChildren` or {class}`Folder.getDescendants`,
this method returns only the descendant objects that are folders. The
results do not include the current folder.

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - depth
  - filter
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - includePathSegment

  ```pycon
  >>> rs = folder.getTree(depth='2')
  >>> len(rs.getResults())
  3
  >>> for folder in rs.getResults().values():
  ...     folder.getTitle()
  ...
  u'subfolder2'
  u'parent test folder'
  u'subfolder'
  ```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Folder.getParent
documentation: The optional filter argument is not yet supported.

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Folder.deleteTree
documentation:
Deletes the folder and all of its descendant objects.

```pycon
>>> resultSet = subFolder.getDescendants()
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
2
>>> subFolder.deleteTree()
```

methods.name: cmislib.domain.Folder.addObject
documentation:
Adds the specified object as a child of this object. No new object is
created. The repository must support multifiling for this to work.

```pycon
>>> sub1 = repo.getObjectByPath("/cmislib/sub1")
>>> sub2 = repo.getObjectByPath("/cmislib/sub2")
>>> doc = sub1.createDocument("testdoc1")
>>> len(sub1.getChildren())
1
>>> len(sub2.getChildren())
0
>>> sub2.addObject(doc)
>>> len(sub2.getChildren())
1
>>> sub2.getChildren()[0].name
u'testdoc1'
```

The following optional arguments are supported:


methods.name: cmislib.domain.ACL.getEntries
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of {class}`ACE` objects for each Access Control
Entry in the ACL. The key value is the ACE principalid.

```pycon
>>> acl = ACL()
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jsmith', 'cmis:write', 'true'))
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jpotts', 'cmis:write', 'true'))
>>> for ace in acl.entries.values():
...     print 'principal:%s has the following '        ...        'permissions...' % ace.principalId
...     for perm in ace.permissions:
...             print perm
...
principal:jpotts has the following permissions...
cmis:write
principal:jsmith has the following permissions...
cmis:write
```

classes.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserBinding

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserBinding.get
documentation:
Does a get against the CMIS service. More than likely, you will not
need to call this method. Instead, let the other objects do it for you.

For example, if you need to get a specific object by object id, try
{class}`Repository.getObject`. If you have a path instead of an object
id, use {class}`Repository.getObjectByPath`. Or, you could start with
the root folder ({class}`Repository.getRootFolder`) and drill down from
there.

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserBinding.post
documentation:
Does a post against the CMIS service. More than likely, you will not
need to call this method. Instead, let the other objects do it for you.

For example, to update the properties on an object, you'd call
{class}`CmisObject.updateProperties`. Or, to check in a document that's
been checked out, you'd call {class}`Document.checkin` on the PWC.

Implements the Browser Binding to communicate with the CMIS server. The
Browser Binding uses only GET and POST. It sends JSON and HTML forms and
gets back JSON.

reset to the full list of properties, call reload with filter set to


```pycon
u'someFolder2'
>>> props = {'cmis:name': 'someFolderFoo'}
>>> folder.updateProperties(props)
<cmislib.model.Folder object at 0x103ab1210>
>>> folder.getName()
u'someFolderFoo'
```

documentation:

```pycon
>>> sub1 = repo.getObjectByPath('/cmislib/sub1')
>>> sub2 = repo.getObjectByPath('/cmislib/sub2')

```

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserCmisObject.delete
documentation:
Deletes this {class}`CmisObject` from the repository. Note that in the
case of a {class}`Folder` object, some repositories will refuse to
delete it if it contains children and some will delete it without
complaint. If what you really want to do is delete the folder and all
of its descendants, use {meth}`~Folder.deleteTree` instead.

```pycon
>>> folder.delete()
```

The optional allVersions argument is supported.

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserRepository.query
documentation:
Returns a list of {class}`CmisObject` objects based on the CMIS
Query Language passed in as the statement. The actual objects
returned will be instances of the appropriate child class based
on the object's base type ID.

In order for the results to be properly instantiated as objects,
make sure you include 'cmis:objectId' as one of the fields in
your select statement, or just use "SELECT ".

If you want the search results to automatically be instantiated with
the appropriate sub-class of {class}`CmisObject` you must either
include cmis:baseTypeId as one of the fields in your select statement
or just use "SELECT ".

```pycon
>>> q = "select * from cmis:document where cmis:name like '%test%'"
>>> resultSet = repo.query(q)
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
1
>>> resultSet.hasNext()
False
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - searchAllVersions
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - maxItems
  - skipCount

```pycon
>>> q = 'select * from cmis:document'
>>> rs = repo.query(q)
>>> len(rs.getResults())
148
>>> rs = repo.query(q, maxItems='5')
>>> len(rs.getResults())
5
>>> rs.hasNext()
True
```

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.createFolder
documentation:
Creates a new {class}`Folder` using the properties provided.
Right now I expect a property called 'cmis:name' but I don't
complain if it isn't there (although the CMIS provider will). If a
cmis:name property isn't provided, the value passed in to the name
argument will be used.

To specify a custom folder type, pass in a property called
cmis:objectTypeId set to the {class}`CmisId` representing the type ID
of the instance you want to create. If you do not pass in an object
type ID, an instance of 'cmis:folder' will be created.

```pycon
>>> subFolder = folder.createFolder('someSubfolder')
>>> subFolder.getName()
u'someSubfolder'
```

The following optional arguments are not supported:
: - policies
  - addACEs
  - removeACEs

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.createDocumentFromString
documentation:
Creates a new document setting the content to the string provided. If
the repository supports unfiled objects, you do not have to pass in
a parent {class}`Folder` otherwise it is required.

```pycon
>>> testFolder.createDocumentFromString(
... 'testdoc3', contentString='hello, world', contentType='text/plain')
```

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.createDocument
documentation:
Creates a new Document object in the repository using
the properties provided.

Right now this is basically the same as createFolder,
but this deals with contentStreams. The common logic should
probably be moved to CmisObject.createObject.

The method will attempt to guess the appropriate content
type and encoding based on the file. To specify it yourself, pass them
in via the contentType and contentEncoding arguments.

```pycon
>>> f = open('250px-Cmis_logo.png', 'rb')
>>> subFolder.createDocument('logo.png', contentFile=f)
<cmislib.model.Document object at 0x10410fa10>
>>> f.close()
```

If you wanted to set one or more properties when creating the doc, pass
in a dict, like this:

```pycon
>>> props = {'cmis:someProp':'someVal'}
>>> f = open('250px-Cmis_logo.png', 'rb')
>>> subFolder.createDocument('logo.png', props, contentFile=f)
<cmislib.model.Document object at 0x10410fa10>
>>> f.close()
```

To specify a custom object type, pass in a property called
cmis:objectTypeId set to the {class}`CmisId` representing the type ID
of the instance you want to create. If you do not pass in an object
type ID, an instance of 'cmis:document' will be created.

The following optional arguments are not yet supported:
: - versioningState
  - policies
  - addACEs
  - removeACEs

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.getChildren
documentation:
Returns a paged {class}`ResultSet`. The result set contains a list of
{class}`CmisObject` objects for each child of the Folder. The actual
type of the object returned depends on the object's CMIS base type id.
For example, the method might return a list that contains both
{class}`Document` objects and {class}`Folder` objects.

```pycon
>>> childrenRS = subFolder.getChildren()
>>> children = childrenRS.getResults()
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - maxItems
  - skipCount
  - orderBy
  - filter
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - includePathSegment

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.getDescendants
documentation:
Gets the descendants of this folder. The descendants are returned as
a paged {class}`ResultSet` object. The result set contains a list of
{class}`CmisObject` objects where the actual type of each object
returned will vary depending on the object's base type id. For example,
the method might return a list that contains both {class}`Document`
objects and {class}`Folder` objects.

The following optional argument is supported:
: - depth. Use depth=-1 for all descendants, which is the default if no
    depth is specified.

```pycon
>>> resultSet = folder.getDescendants()
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
105
>>> resultSet = folder.getDescendants(depth=1)
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
103
```

The following optional arguments *may* also work but haven't been
tested:

> - filter
> - includeRelationships
> - renditionFilter
> - includeAllowableActions
> - includePathSegment

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.getTree
documentation:
Unlike {class}`Folder.getChildren` or {class}`Folder.getDescendants`,
this method returns only the descendant objects that are folders. The
results do not include the current folder.

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - depth
  - filter
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - includePathSegment

  ```pycon
  >>> rs = folder.getTree(depth='2')
  >>> len(rs.getResults())
  3
  >>> for folder in rs.getResults().values():
  ...     folder.getTitle()
  ...
  u'subfolder2'
  u'parent test folder'
  u'subfolder'
  ```

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.getParent
documentation: The optional filter argument is not yet supported.

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.deleteTree
documentation:
Deletes the folder and all of its descendant objects.

```pycon
>>> resultSet = subFolder.getDescendants()
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
2
>>> subFolder.deleteTree()
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - allVersions
  - unfileObjects
  - continueOnFailure

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.addObject
documentation:
Adds the specified object as a child of this object. No new object is
created. The repository must support multifiling for this to work.

```pycon
>>> sub1 = repo.getObjectByPath("/cmislib/sub1")
>>> sub2 = repo.getObjectByPath("/cmislib/sub2")
>>> doc = sub1.createDocument("testdoc1")
>>> len(sub1.getChildren())
1
>>> len(sub2.getChildren())
0
>>> sub2.addObject(doc)
>>> len(sub2.getChildren())
1
>>> sub2.getChildren()[0].name
u'testdoc1'
```

The following optional arguments are NOT supported:
: - allVersions

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.removeObject
documentation:
Removes the specified object from this folder. The repository must
support unfiling for this to work.

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserFolder.getPaths
documentation:
Returns the paths as a list of strings. The spec says folders cannot
be multi-filed, so this should always be one value. We return a list
to be symmetric with the same method in {class}`Document`.

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserACL.addEntry
documentation:
Adds an {class}`ACE` entry to the ACL.

```pycon
>>> acl = folder.getACL()
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jpotts', 'cmis:read', 'true'))
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jsmith', 'cmis:write', 'true'))
>>> acl.getEntries()
{u'GROUP_EVERYONE': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731410>,
u'jdoe': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731150>,
'jpotts': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a22d0>,
'jsmith': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a2210>}
```

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserACL.removeEntry
documentation:
Removes the {class}`ACE` entry given a specific principalId.

```pycon
>>> acl.getEntries()
{u'GROUP_EVERYONE': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731410>,
u'jdoe': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731150>,
'jpotts': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a22d0>,
'jsmith': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a2210>}
>>> acl.removeEntry('jsmith')
>>> acl.getEntries()
{u'GROUP_EVERYONE': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731410>,
 u'jdoe': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731150>,
 'jpotts': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a22d0>}
```

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserACL.clearEntries
documentation:
Clears all {class}`ACE` entries from the ACL and removes the internal
XML representation of the ACL.

```pycon
>>> acl = ACL()
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jsmith', 'cmis:write', 'true'))
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jpotts', 'cmis:write', 'true'))
>>> acl.entries
{'jpotts': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1012c7310>,
 'jsmith': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100528490>}
>>> acl.clearEntries()
>>> acl.entries
```

methods.name: cmislib.browser.binding.BrowserACL.getEntries
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of {class}`ACE` objects for each Access Control
Entry in the ACL. The key value is the ACE principalid.

```pycon
>>> acl = ACL()
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jsmith', 'cmis:write', 'true'))
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jpotts', 'cmis:write', 'true'))
>>> for ace in acl.entries.values():
...     print('principal:%s has the following permissions...'
...           ''% ace.principalId)
...     for perm in ace.permissions:
...             print perm
...
principal:jpotts has the following permissions...
cmis:write
principal:jsmith has the following permissions...
cmis:write
```

classes.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubBinding

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubBinding.get
documentation:
Does a get against the CMIS service. More than likely, you will not
need to call this method. Instead, let the other objects do it for you.

For example, if you need to get a specific object by object id, try
{class}`Repository.getObject`. If you have a path instead of an object
id, use {class}`Repository.getObjectByPath`. Or, you could start with
the root folder ({class}`Repository.getRootFolder`) and drill down from
there.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubBinding.post
documentation:
Does a post against the CMIS service. More than likely, you will not
need to call this method. Instead, let the other objects do it for you.

For example, to update the properties on an object, you'd call
{class}`CmisObject.updateProperties`. Or, to check in a document that's
been checked out, you'd call {class}`Document.checkin` on the PWC.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubBinding.put
documentation:
Does a put against the CMIS service. More than likely, you will not
need to call this method. Instead, let the other objects do it for you.

For example, to update the properties on an object, you'd call
{class}`CmisObject.updateProperties`. Or, to check in a document that's
been checked out, you'd call {class}`Document.checkin` on the PWC.

The binding responsible for talking to the CMIS server via the AtomPub
Publishing Protocol.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubCmisObject.getProperties
documentation:
Returns a dict of the object's properties. If CMIS returns an
empty element for a property, the property will be in the
dict with a value of None.

```pycon
>>> props = doc.getProperties()
>>> for p in props:
...     print "%s: %s" % (p, props[p])
...
cmis:contentStreamMimeType: text/html
cmis:creationDate: 2009-12-15T09:45:35.369-06:00
cmis:baseTypeId: cmis:document
cmis:isLatestMajorVersion: false
cmis:isImmutable: false
cmis:isMajorVersion: false
cmis:objectId:
    workspace://SpacesStore/dc26102b-e312-471b-b2af-91bfb0225339
```

The optional filter argument is not yet implemented.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubCmisObject.getName
documentation:
Returns the value of cmis:name from the getProperties() dictionary.
We don't need a getter for every standard CMIS property, but name
is a pretty common one so it seems to make sense.

```pycon
>>> doc.getName()
u'system-overview.html'
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubCmisObject.updateProperties
documentation:
Updates the properties of an object with the properties provided.
Only provide the set of properties that need to be updated.

```pycon
>>> folder = repo.getObjectByPath('/someFolder2')
>>> folder.getName()
u'someFolder2'
>>> props = {'cmis:name': 'someFolderFoo'}
>>> folder.updateProperties(props)
<cmislib.model.Folder object at 0x103ab1210>
>>> folder.getName()
u'someFolderFoo'
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubCmisObject.move
documentation:
Moves an object from the source folder to the target folder.

```pycon
>>> sub1 = repo.getObjectByPath('/cmislib/sub1')
>>> sub2 = repo.getObjectByPath('/cmislib/sub2')
>>> doc = repo.getObjectByPath('/cmislib/sub1/testdoc1')
>>> doc.move(sub1, sub2)
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubCmisObject.delete
documentation:
Deletes this {class}`CmisObject` from the repository. Note that in the
case of a {class}`Folder` object, some repositories will refuse to
delete it if it contains children and some will delete it without
complaint. If what you really want to do is delete the folder and all
of its descendants, use {meth}`~Folder.deleteTree` instead.

```pycon
>>> folder.delete()
```

The optional allVersions argument is supported.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubRepository.getSupportedPermissions
documentation:
Returns the value of the cmis:supportedPermissions element. Valid
values are:

> - basic: indicates that the CMIS Basic permissions are supported
> - repository: indicates that repository specific permissions are
>   : supported
> - both: indicates that both CMIS basic permissions and repository
>   : specific permissions are supported

```pycon
>>> repo.supportedPermissions
u'both'
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubRepository.getPermissionDefinitions
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of permission definitions for this repository. The
key is the permission string or technical name of the permission
and the value is the permission description.

```pycon
>>> for permDef in repo.permissionDefinitions:
...     print permDef
...
cmis:all
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.LinkChildren
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/content/1.0}folder.Consumer
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/security/1.0}All.All
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.CreateAssociations
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.FullControl
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.AddChildren
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.ReadAssociations
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/content/1.0}folder.Editor
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/content/1.0}cmobject.Editor
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.DeleteAssociations
cmis:read
cmis:write
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubRepository.getPermissionMap
documentation:
Returns a dictionary representing the permission mapping table where
each key is a permission key string and each value is a list of one or
more permissions the principal must have to perform the operation.

```pycon
>>> for (k,v) in repo.permissionMap.items():
...     print 'To do this: %s, you must have these perms:' % k
...     for perm in v:
...             print perm
...
To do this: canCreateFolder.Folder, you must have these perms:
cmis:all
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.CreateChildren
To do this: canAddToFolder.Folder, you must have these perms:
cmis:all
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.CreateChildren
To do this: canDelete.Object, you must have these perms:
cmis:all
{http://www.alfresco.org/model/system/1.0}base.DeleteNode
To do this: canCheckin.Document, you must have these perms:

{http://www.alfresco.org/model/content/1.0}lockable.CheckIn
```

documentation:
Returns a list of {class}`ObjectType` objects corresponding to the
child types of the type specified by the typeId.

If no typeId is provided, the result will be the same as calling
`self.getTypeDefinitions`

These optional arguments are current unsupported:
: - includePropertyDefinitions
  - maxItems
  - skipCount

```pycon
>>> baseTypes = repo.getTypeChildren()
>>> for baseType in baseTypes:
...     print baseType.getTypeId()
...
cmis:folder
cmis:relationship
cmis:document
cmis:policy
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubRepository.getTypeDescendants
documentation:
Returns a list of {class}`ObjectType` objects corresponding to the
descendant types of the type specified by the typeId.

If no typeId is provided, the repository's "typesdescendants" URL
will be called to determine the list of descendant types.

```pycon
>>> allTypes = repo.getTypeDescendants()
>>> for aType in allTypes:
...     print aType.getTypeId()
...
cmis:folder
F:cm:systemfolder
F:act:savedactionfolder
F:app:configurations
F:fm:forums
F:wcm:avmfolder
F:wcm:avmplainfolder
F:wca:webfolder
F:wcm:avmlayeredfolder
F:st:site
F:app:glossary
F:fm:topic
```

These optional arguments are supported:
: - depth
  - includePropertyDefinitions

```pycon
>>> types = repo.getTypeDescendants('cmis:folder')
>>> len(types)
17
>>> types = repo.getTypeDescendants('cmis:folder', depth=1)
>>> len(types)
12
>>> types = repo.getTypeDescendants('cmis:folder', depth=2)
>>> len(types)
17
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubRepository.query
documentation:
Returns a list of {class}`CmisObject` objects based on the CMIS
Query Language passed in as the statement. The actual objects
returned will be instances of the appropriate child class based
on the object's base type ID.

In order for the results to be properly instantiated as objects,
make sure you include 'cmis:objectId' as one of the fields in
your select statement, or just use "SELECT ".

If you want the search results to automatically be instantiated with
the appropriate sub-class of {class}`CmisObject` you must either
include cmis:baseTypeId as one of the fields in your select statement
or just use "SELECT ".

```pycon
>>> q = "select * from cmis:document where cmis:name like '%test%'"
>>> resultSet = repo.query(q)
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
1
>>> resultSet.hasNext()
False
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - searchAllVersions
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - maxItems
  - skipCount

```pycon
>>> q = 'select * from cmis:document'
>>> rs = repo.query(q)
>>> len(rs.getResults())
148
>>> rs = repo.query(q, maxItems='5')
>>> len(rs.getResults())
5
>>> rs.hasNext()
True
```

```pycon
>>> for changeEntry in rs:
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubRepository.createDocumentFromString
documentation:
Creates a new document setting the content to the string provided. If
the repository supports unfiled objects, you do not have to pass in
a parent {class}`Folder` otherwise it is required.

This method is essentially a convenience method that wraps your string
with a StringIO and then calls createDocument.

```pycon
>>> repo.createDocumentFromString(
...    'testdoc5', parentFolder=testFolder,
...    contentString='Hello, World!', contentType='text/plain')
<cmislib.model.Document object at 0x101352ed0>
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubRepository.createDocument
documentation:
Creates a new {class}`Document` object. If the repository
supports unfiled objects, you do not have to pass in
a parent {class}`Folder` otherwise it is required.

To create a document with an associated contentFile, pass in a
File object. The method will attempt to guess the appropriate content
type and encoding based on the file. To specify it yourself, pass them
in via the contentType and contentEncoding arguments.

```pycon
>>> f = open('sample-a.pdf', 'rb')
>>> doc = folder.createDocument('sample-a.pdf', contentFile=f)
<cmislib.model.Document object at 0x105be5e10>
>>> f.close()
>>> doc.getTitle()
u'sample-a.pdf'
```

The following optional arguments are not currently supported:
: - versioningState
  - policies
  - addACEs
  - removeACEs

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubRepository.getCollection
documentation:
Returns a list of objects returned for the specified collection.

If the query collection is requested, an exception will be raised.
That collection isn't meant to be retrieved.

If the types collection is specified, the method returns the result of
`getTypeDefinitions` and ignores any optional params passed in.

```pycon
>>> from cmislib.atompub.atompub_binding import TYPES_COLL
>>> types = repo.getCollection(TYPES_COLL)
>>> len(types)
4
>>> types[0].getTypeId()
u'cmis:folder'
```

Otherwise, the collection URL is invoked, and a {class}`ResultSet` is
returned.

```pycon
>>> from cmislib.atompub.atompub_binding import CHECKED_OUT_COLL
>>> resultSet = repo.getCollection(CHECKED_OUT_COLL)
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
1
```

and then converts the resulting XML into a dictionary of
{class}`CmisObject` objects or its appropriate sub-type.

The results are kept around to facilitate repeated calls without moving
the cursor.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubResultSet.reload
documentation:
Re-invokes the self link for the current set of results.

```pycon
>>> resultSet = repo.getCollection(CHECKED_OUT_COLL)
>>> resultSet.reload()
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubResultSet.getResults

```pycon
>>> resultSet = repo.getCheckedOutDocs()

False
>>> for result in resultSet.getResults():

...
<cmislib.model.Document object at 0x104851810>
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubResultSet.hasObject

classes.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.checkout
documentation:
Performs a checkout on the {class}`Document` and returns the
Private Working Copy (PWC), which is also an instance of
{class}`Document`

```pycon
>>> doc.getObjectId()
u'workspace://SpacesStore/f0c8b90f-bec0-4405-8b9c-2ab570589808;1.0'
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
>>> pwc = doc.checkout()
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
True
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.cancelCheckout
documentation:
Cancels the checkout of this object by retrieving the Private Working
Copy (PWC) and then deleting it. After the PWC is deleted, this object
will be reloaded to update properties related to a checkout.

```pycon
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
True
>>> doc.cancelCheckout()
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.getPrivateWorkingCopy
documentation:
Retrieves the object using the object ID in the property:
cmis:versionSeriesCheckedOutId then uses getObject to instantiate
the object.

```pycon
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
>>> doc.checkout()
<cmislib.model.Document object at 0x103a25ad0>
>>> pwc = doc.getPrivateWorkingCopy()
>>> pwc.getTitle()
u'sample-b (Working Copy).pdf'
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.isCheckedOut
documentation:
Returns true if the document is checked out.

```pycon
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
True
>>> doc.cancelCheckout()
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.getCheckedOutBy
documentation:
Returns the ID who currently has the document checked out.
\>>> pwc = doc.checkout()
\>>> pwc.getCheckedOutBy()
u'admin'

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.checkin
documentation:
Checks in this {class}`Document` which must be a private
working copy (PWC).

```pycon
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
>>> pwc = doc.checkout()
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
True
>>> pwc.checkin()
<cmislib.model.Document object at 0x103a8ae90>
>>> doc.isCheckedOut()
False
```

The following optional arguments are NOT supported:
: - policies
  - addACEs
  - removeACEs

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.getLatestVersion
documentation:
Returns a {class}`Document` object representing the latest version in
the version series.

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - major
  - filter
  - includeRelationships
  - includePolicyIds
  - renditionFilter
  - includeACL
  - includeAllowableActions

```pycon
>>> latestDoc = doc.getLatestVersion()
>>> latestDoc.getProperties()['cmis:versionLabel']
u'2.1'
>>> latestDoc = doc.getLatestVersion(major='false')
>>> latestDoc.getProperties()['cmis:versionLabel']
u'2.1'
>>> latestDoc = doc.getLatestVersion(major='true')
>>> latestDoc.getProperties()['cmis:versionLabel']
u'2.0'
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.getPropertiesOfLatestVersion
documentation:
Like {class}`^CmisObject.getProperties`, returns a dict of properties
from the latest version of this object in the version series.

The optional major and filter arguments are supported.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.getAllVersions
documentation:
Returns a {class}`ResultSet` of document objects for the entire
version history of this object, including any PWC's.

The optional filter and includeAllowableActions are
supported.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.getContentStream
documentation:
Returns the CMIS service response from invoking the 'enclosure' link.

```pycon
>>> doc.getName()
u'sample-b.pdf'
>>> o = open('tmp.pdf', 'wb')
>>> result = doc.getContentStream()
>>> o.write(result.read())
>>> result.close()
>>> o.close()
>>> import os.path
>>> os.path.getsize('tmp.pdf')
117248
```

The optional streamId argument is not yet supported.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.setContentStream
documentation:
Sets the content stream on this object.

The following optional arguments are not yet supported:
 - overwriteFlag=None

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.deleteContentStream
documentation: Delete's the content stream associated with this object.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubDocument.getPaths
documentation:
Returns the Document's paths by asking for the parents with the
includeRelativePathSegment flag set to true, then concats the value
of cmis:path with the relativePathSegment.
An object typically associated with file content.

classes.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.createFolder
documentation:
Creates a new {class}`Folder` using the properties provided.
Right now I expect a property called 'cmis:name' but I don't
complain if it isn't there (although the CMIS provider will). If a
cmis:name property isn't provided, the value passed in to the name
argument will be used.

To specify a custom folder type, pass in a property called
cmis:objectTypeId set to the {class}`CmisId` representing the type ID
of the instance you want to create. If you do not pass in an object
type ID, an instance of 'cmis:folder' will be created.

```pycon
>>> subFolder = folder.createFolder('someSubfolder')
>>> subFolder.getName()
u'someSubfolder'
```

The following optional arguments are not supported:
: - policies
  - addACEs
  - removeACEs

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.createDocumentFromString
documentation:
Creates a new document setting the content to the string provided. If
the repository supports unfiled objects, you do not have to pass in
a parent {class}`Folder` otherwise it is required.

This method is essentially a convenience method that wraps your string
with a StringIO and then calls createDocument.

```pycon
>>> testFolder.createDocumentFromString(
...    'testdoc3', contentString='hello, world',
...    contentType='text/plain')
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.createDocument
documentation:
Creates a new Document object in the repository using
the properties provided.

Right now this is basically the same as createFolder,
but this deals with contentStreams. The common logic should
probably be moved to CmisObject.createObject.

The method will attempt to guess the appropriate content
type and encoding based on the file. To specify it yourself, pass them
in via the contentType and contentEncoding arguments.

```pycon
>>> f = open('250px-Cmis_logo.png', 'rb')
>>> subFolder.createDocument('logo.png', contentFile=f)
<cmislib.model.Document object at 0x10410fa10>
>>> f.close()
```

If you wanted to set one or more properties when creating the doc, pass
in a dict, like this:

```pycon
>>> props = {'cmis:someProp':'someVal'}
>>> f = open('250px-Cmis_logo.png', 'rb')
>>> subFolder.createDocument('logo.png', props, contentFile=f)
<cmislib.model.Document object at 0x10410fa10>
>>> f.close()
```

To specify a custom object type, pass in a property called
cmis:objectTypeId set to the {class}`CmisId` representing the type ID
of the instance you want to create. If you do not pass in an object
type ID, an instance of 'cmis:document' will be created.

The following optional arguments are not yet supported:
: - versioningState
  - policies
  - addACEs
  - removeACEs

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.getChildren
documentation:
Returns a paged {class}`ResultSet`. The result set contains a list of
{class}`CmisObject` objects for each child of the Folder. The actual
type of the object returned depends on the object's CMIS base type id.
For example, the method might return a list that contains both
{class}`Document` objects and {class}`Folder` objects.

```pycon
>>> childrenRS = subFolder.getChildren()
>>> children = childrenRS.getResults()
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - maxItems
  - skipCount
  - orderBy
  - filter
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - includePathSegment

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.getChildrenLink
documentation: Gets the Atom link that knows how to return this object's children.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.getDescendantsLink
documentation:
Returns the 'down' link of type `CMIS_TREE_TYPE`

```pycon
>>> folder.getDescendantsLink()
u'http://localhost:8080/alfresco/s/cmis/s/workspace:SpacesStore/i/
86f6bf54-f0e8-4a72-8cb1-213599ba086c/descendants'
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.getDescendants
documentation:
Gets the descendants of this folder. The descendants are returned as
a paged {class}`ResultSet` object. The result set contains a list of
{class}`CmisObject` objects where the actual type of each object
returned will vary depending on the object's base type id. For example,
the method might return a list that contains both {class}`Document`
objects and {class}`Folder` objects.

The following optional argument is supported:
: - depth. Use depth=-1 for all descendants, which is the default if no
    depth is specified.

```pycon
>>> resultSet = folder.getDescendants()
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
105
>>> resultSet = folder.getDescendants(depth=1)
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
103
```

The following optional arguments *may* also work but haven't been
tested:

> - filter
> - includeRelationships
> - renditionFilter
> - includeAllowableActions
> - includePathSegment

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.getTree
documentation:
Unlike {class}`Folder.getChildren` or {class}`Folder.getDescendants`,
this method returns only the descendant objects that are folders. The
results do not include the current folder.

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - depth
  - filter
  - includeRelationships
  - renditionFilter
  - includeAllowableActions
  - includePathSegment

  ```pycon
  >>> rs = folder.getTree(depth='2')
  >>> len(rs.getResults())
  3
  >>> for folder in rs.getResults().values():
  ...     folder.getTitle()
  ...
  u'subfolder2'
  u'parent test folder'
  u'subfolder'
  ```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.getParent
documentation:
This is not yet implemented.

The optional filter argument is not yet supported.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.deleteTree
documentation:
Deletes the folder and all of its descendant objects.

```pycon
>>> resultSet = subFolder.getDescendants()
>>> len(resultSet.getResults())
2
>>> subFolder.deleteTree()
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - allVersions
  - unfileObjects
  - continueOnFailure

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.addObject
documentation:
Adds the specified object as a child of this object. No new object is
created. The repository must support multifiling for this to work.

```pycon
>>> sub1 = repo.getObjectByPath("/cmislib/sub1")
>>> sub2 = repo.getObjectByPath("/cmislib/sub2")
>>> doc = sub1.createDocument("testdoc1")
>>> len(sub1.getChildren())
1
>>> len(sub2.getChildren())
0
>>> sub2.addObject(doc)
>>> len(sub2.getChildren())
1
>>> sub2.getChildren()[0].name
u'testdoc1'
```

The following optional arguments are supported:
: - allVersions

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.removeObject
documentation:
Removes the specified object from this folder. The repository must
support unfiling for this to work.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubFolder.getPaths
documentation:
Returns the paths as a list of strings. The spec says folders cannot
be multi-filed, so this should always be one value. We return a list
to be symmetric with the same method in {class}`Document`.
A container object that can hold other {class}`CmisObject` objects

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubACL.__init__
documentation:
Constructor. Pass in either a dict of {class}`ACE` objects keyed to the
principalId or the XML representation of the ACL.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubACL.addEntry
documentation:
Adds an {class}`ACE` entry to the ACL.

The default for direct is True but you can override it if needed.

```pycon
>>> acl = folder.getACL()
>>> acl.addEntry('jpotts', 'cmis:read')
>>> acl.addEntry('jsmith', 'cmis:write')
>>> acl.getEntries()
{u'GROUP_EVERYONE': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731410>,
 u'jdoe': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731150>,
 'jpotts': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a22d0>,
 'jsmith': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a2210>}
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubACL.removeEntry
documentation:
Removes the {class}`ACE` entry given a specific principalId. If a given
principalId has more than one permission, calling removeEntry will
remove the entry completely.

```pycon
>>> acl.getEntries()
{u'GROUP_EVERYONE': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731410>,
 u'jdoe': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731150>,
 'jpotts': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a22d0>,
 'jsmith': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a2210>}
>>> acl.removeEntry('jsmith')
>>> acl.getEntries()
{u'GROUP_EVERYONE': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731410>,
 u'jdoe': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100731150>,
 'jpotts': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1005a22d0>}
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubACL.clearEntries
documentation:
Clears all {class}`ACE` entries from the ACL and removes the internal
XML representation of the ACL.

```pycon
>>> acl = ACL()
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jsmith', 'cmis:write'))
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jpotts', 'cmis:write'))
>>> acl.entries
{'jpotts': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x1012c7310>,
 'jsmith': <cmislib.model.ACE object at 0x100528490>}
>>> acl.getXmlDoc()
<xml.dom.minidom.Document instance at 0x1012cbb90>
>>> acl.clearEntries()
>>> acl.entries
>>> acl.getXmlDoc()
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubACL.getEntries
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of {class}`ACE` objects for each Access Control
Entry in the ACL. The key value is the ACE principalid.

```pycon
>>> acl = ACL()
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jsmith', 'cmis:write'))
>>> acl.addEntry(ACE('jpotts', 'cmis:write'))
>>> for ace in acl.entries.values():
...     print 'principal:%s has the following '        ...        'permissions...' % ace.principalId
...     for perm in ace.permissions:
...             print perm
...
principal:jpotts has the following permissions...
cmis:write
principal:jsmith has the following permissions...
cmis:write
```

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubChangeEntry.getObjectId
documentation: Returns the object ID of the object that changed.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubChangeEntry.getChangeType
documentation:
Returns the type of change that occurred. The resulting value must be
one of:

 - created
 - updated
 - deleted
 - security

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubChangeEntry.getACL
documentation: Gets the {class}`ACL` object that is included with this Change Entry.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubChangeEntry.getChangeTime
documentation: Returns a datetime object representing the time the change occurred.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubChangeEntry.getProperties
documentation:
Returns the properties of the change entry. Note that depending on the
capabilities of the repository ("capabilityChanges") the list may not
include the actual property values that changed.

methods.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubChangeEntry._getLink
documentation:
Returns the HREF attribute of an Atom link element for the
specified rel.

Represents a change log entry. Retrieve a list of change entries via
{meth}`Repository.getContentChanges`.

```pycon
...     changeEntry.objectId
...     changeEntry.id
...     changeEntry.changeType
...     changeEntry.changeTime
...
```

{class}`ChangeEntry` objects. The parent class assumes children of
{class}`CmisObject` which doesn't work for ChangeEntries.

classes.name: cmislib.atompub.binding.AtomPubCmisId
documentation:
This is a marker class to be used for Strings that are used as CMIS ID's.
Making the objects instances of this class makes it easier to create the
Atom entry XML with the appropriate type, ie, cmis:propertyId, instead of
cmis:propertyString.
